---
title: "Kendrick_Lyrics"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r}
library(geniusr)
library(tidyverse)
library(httr)
library(rvest)

# Set your API token (the one you get from genius.com/api-clients)
genius_token <- "i7y_xn25vfaNW2mp-EccxzdyENT7OtXmjUlK4-f5lyzT11Vw-AnFBeDEo5AqsJuQ"
Sys.setenv(GENIUS_API_TOKEN = genius_token)
```

```{r}
# Install the patched fork directly from the community member who fixed it
remotes::install_github("giovanni-cutri/geniusr")

# Reload the library
library(geniusr)
library(tidyverse)
```

```{r}
lyrics <- read_csv("discog_data.csv")
```

```{r}
# Get rid of his first album -- not my favorite
lyrics_clean <- lyrics %>% 
  filter(album != "Overly Dedicated")
```

```{r}
# Add his newest album and my leak playlist, not included in the Kaggle dataset

# 1. Get the tracklist for GNX
# This returns a table with song names and IDs
gnx_tracks <- get_album_tracklist_search(artist_name = "Kendrick Lamar", album_name = "GNX")
```

```{r}
# 1. The Fixed Scraper
scrape_modern_genius <- function(url) {
  tryCatch({
    browser_mask <- user_agent("Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/120.0.0.0 Safari/537.36")
    response <- GET(url, browser_mask)
    page <- read_html(response)
    
    # Using the exact data attribute you found, and the correct text extraction function
    lyrics <- page %>% 
      html_elements('div[data-lyrics-container="true"]') %>% 
      html_text2() %>%  # html_text2() natively handles line breaks correctly
      paste(collapse = "\n") 
    
    return(lyrics)
  }, error = function(e) {
    # If it fails, print the actual reason!
    print(paste("Scrape failed:", e$message))
    return(NA)
  }) 
}
```

```{r}
# 1. SCRAPE: Pull the fresh lyrics to get our line breaks back
gnx_lyrics <- gnx_tracks %>%
  mutate(lyrics = map_chr(song_lyrics_url, scrape_modern_genius)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(lyrics) & lyrics != "") %>% 
  mutate(album = "GNX", popularity = 85) %>%
  rename(track_name = song_title) %>%
  mutate(track_name = str_remove(track_name, "\\s?[\\(\\[]?(?i)f(ea)?t\\..*") %>% str_trim()) %>% 
  select(track_name, album, lyrics, popularity)

# 2. CLEAN: The gentle pipeline
gnx_nlp_ready <- gnx_lyrics %>%
  mutate(
    # A. Chop off the Genius metadata honeypot 
    # (?s) ensures the regex works across your newly restored line breaks!
    lyrics = if_else(
      str_detect(lyrics, "Read More"),
      str_remove(lyrics, "(?s)^.*?Read More\\s*"), 
      str_remove(lyrics, "(?s)^.*?Lyrics\\s*")     
    ),
    
    # B. Remove the [Chorus] and [Verse] tags
    lyrics = str_replace_all(lyrics, "\\[.*?\\]", ""),
    
    # C. Fix the smashed words ("teenagerShe" -> "teenager She")
    lyrics = str_replace_all(lyrics, "([a-z])([A-Z])", "\\1 \\2"),
    
    # D. Fix smashed punctuation ("wait!No" -> "wait! No")
    lyrics = str_replace_all(lyrics, ",([a-zA-Z])", ", \\1"),
    lyrics = str_replace_all(lyrics, "([.!?)])([a-zA-Z])", "\\1 \\2"),
    
    # E. The Gentle Clean (Removes double spaces, leaves \n alone!)
    lyrics = str_replace_all(lyrics, " {2,}", " ") %>% str_trim(),
    
    # Fix acronyms smashed into a capitalized word ("TVMy" -> "TV My")
    lyrics = str_replace_all(lyrics, "([A-Z])([A-Z][a-z])", "\\1 \\2"),
    
    # Fix numbers smashed into letters ("1970The" -> "1970 The")
    lyrics = str_replace_all(lyrics, "([0-9])([A-Z])", "\\1 \\2"),
    
    # Find any sequence of 2 or more line breaks and replace them with just 1
    lyrics = str_replace_all(lyrics, "\n{2,}", "\n")
  )

# 3. View the result!
head(gnx_nlp_ready)
```

```{r}
# Creating the leaks dataframe from the playlist
leaks_tracks <- tibble(
  song_name = c(
    "Cartoon and Cereal",
    "Money Without Me",
    "Look Out For Detox",
    "The Jig Is Up (Dump'n)",
    "Control", 
    "Westside, Right On Time",
    "The Heart Pt. 3",
    "The Heart Part 4",
    "Black Friday",
    "I'm Better",
    "Handsome",
    "Watch The Party Die",
    "Abortion Money",
    "Prayer",
    "Swimming Pools (Drank)", # Kaggle dataset did not come through here :(
    "Father Time" # Also had N95 lyrics here, oddly
  ),
  song_lyrics_url = c(
    "https://genius.com/Kendrick-lamar-cartoon-and-cereal-lyrics",
    "https://genius.com/Kendrick-lamar-money-without-me-lyrics",
    "https://genius.com/Kendrick-lamar-look-out-for-detox-lyrics",
    "https://genius.com/Kendrick-lamar-the-jig-is-up-dumpn-lyrics",
    "https://genius.com/Big-sean-control-lyrics", 
    "https://genius.com/Kendrick-lamar-westside-right-on-time-lyrics",
    "https://genius.com/Kendrick-lamar-the-heart-pt-3-will-you-let-it-die-lyrics",
    "https://genius.com/Kendrick-lamar-the-heart-part-4-lyrics",
    "https://genius.com/Kendrick-lamar-black-friday-lyrics",
    "https://genius.com/Kendrick-lamar-im-better-lyrics",
    "https://genius.com/Kendrick-lamar-handsome-lyrics",
    "https://genius.com/Kendrick-lamar-watch-the-party-die-lyrics",
    "https://genius.com/Kendrick-lamar-abortion-money-lyrics",
    "https://genius.com/Kendrick-lamar-prayer-lyrics",
    "https://genius.com/Kendrick-lamar-swimming-pools-drank-lyrics",
    "https://genius.com/Kendrick-lamar-father-time-lyrics"
  )
)

# View the table
print(leaks_tracks)
```

```{r}
# 1. RE-SCRAPE: Pull the fresh lyrics to get our line breaks back
leaks_lyrics <- leaks_tracks %>%
  mutate(lyrics = map_chr(song_lyrics_url, scrape_modern_genius)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(lyrics) & lyrics != "") %>% 
  mutate(album = "NATION", popularity = 85) %>%
  rename(track_name = song_name) %>%
  select(track_name, album, lyrics, popularity)

# 2. CLEAN: The gentle pipeline
leaks_nlp_ready <- leaks_lyrics %>%
  mutate(
    # A. Chop off the Genius metadata honeypot 
    # (?s) ensures the regex works across your newly restored line breaks!
    lyrics = if_else(
      str_detect(lyrics, "Read More"),
      str_remove(lyrics, "(?s)^.*?Read More\\s*"), 
      str_remove(lyrics, "(?s)^.*?Lyrics\\s*")     
    ),
    
    # B. Remove the [Chorus] and [Verse] tags
    lyrics = str_replace_all(lyrics, "\\[.*?\\]", ""),
    
    # C. Fix the smashed words ("teenagerShe" -> "teenager She")
    lyrics = str_replace_all(lyrics, "([a-z])([A-Z])", "\\1 \\2"),
    
    # D. Fix smashed punctuation ("wait!No" -> "wait! No")
    lyrics = str_replace_all(lyrics, ",([a-zA-Z])", ", \\1"),
    lyrics = str_replace_all(lyrics, "([.!?)])([a-zA-Z])", "\\1 \\2"),
    
    # E. The Gentle Clean (Removes double spaces, leaves \n alone!)
    lyrics = str_replace_all(lyrics, " {2,}", " ") %>% str_trim(),
    
    # Fix acronyms smashed into a capitalized word ("TVMy" -> "TV My")
    lyrics = str_replace_all(lyrics, "([A-Z])([A-Z][a-z])", "\\1 \\2"),
    
    # Fix numbers smashed into letters ("1970The" -> "1970 The")
    lyrics = str_replace_all(lyrics, "([0-9])([A-Z])", "\\1 \\2"),
    
    # Find any sequence of 2 or more line breaks and replace them with just 1
    lyrics = str_replace_all(lyrics, "\n{2,}", "\n")
  )

# 3. View the result!
head(leaks_nlp_ready)
```

```{r}
# Merge the datasets
leaks_nlp_ready$album[15] = "good kid, m.A.A.d city"
lyrics_clean$album[87] = "NATION"
lyrics_clean <- lyrics_clean %>% 
  filter(track_name != "Swimming Pools") %>% 
  #filter(album != "Diss Tracks") %>% 
  #filter(track_name != "The Recipe" & track_name != "Black Boy Fly" & track_name != "Now Or Never" & track_name != "The Heart Part 5" & track_name != "Swimming Pools") %>% 
  select(track_name, album, lyrics, popularity)
full_albums <- rbind(lyrics_clean, gnx_nlp_ready, leaks_nlp_ready) %>% 
  mutate(
    # "\\S+" tells R to count every cluster of non-whitespace characters as one word
    word_count = str_count(lyrics, "\\S+")
  ) %>% 
  mutate(era = case_when(
    album %in% c("Section.80", "good kid, m.A.A.d city") ~ "early",
    album %in% c("To Pimp A Butterfly", "untitled unmastered.", "DAMN.") ~ "middle",
    album %in% c("Mr. Morale & The Big Steppers", "Diss Tracks", "NATION", "GNX") ~ "late",
    TRUE ~ "unknown"
  )) %>% 
  mutate(era = case_when(
    Id %in% c("Cartoon and Cereal", "Money Without Me", "Look Out For Detox", 
              "The Jig Is Up (Dump'n)", "Control", "Westside, Right On Time", 
              "The Heart Pt. 3") ~ "early",
    Id %in% c("The Heart Part 4", "Black Friday") ~ "middle",
    Id %in% c("6:16 in LA", "I'm Better", "Handsome", "Watch The Party Die", 
              "Abortion Money", "Prayer") ~ "late",
    TRUE ~ era # Keep the existing era for all other non-NATION tracks
  ))

# Save to CSV
write_csv(full_albums, "kendrick_master_dataset.csv")
```
